test_papers.title: TLIF versus PLIF for Lumbar Spinal Stenosis: A Randomized Controlled Trial
test_papers.abstract: Background: Both TLIF and PLIF are effective fusion techniques for lumbar stenosis. This RCT compares clinical and radiological outcomes. Methods: 120 patients were randomized to TLIF (n=60) or PLIF (n=60). Results: Fusion rate at 2 years was 92% in TLIF vs 85% in PLIF (p=0.01). VAS improved from 7.2 to 2.3 in TLIF vs 7.4 to 2.8 in PLIF (p=0.04). ODI improved from 54% to 22% in TLIF vs 56% to 28% in PLIF (p=0.03). Complication rate was lower in TLIF (8% vs 15%, p=0.02). Conclusion: TLIF shows superior fusion rate and lower complication rate compared to PLIF for lumbar stenosis.

test_papers.title: Pedicle Subtraction Osteotomy for Severe Sagittal Imbalance in Adult Spinal Deformity
test_papers.abstract: Background: PSO is a powerful correction technique for severe sagittal imbalance. Methods: 85 consecutive patients with severe sagittal imbalance (SVA > 100mm) underwent PSO. Mean age was 68 years. Results: SVA improved from 152mm to 42mm (p<0.001). PI-LL mismatch improved from 48° to 8° (p<0.001). Cobb angle corrected from 42° to 18° (p<0.001). SRS-22 score improved from 2.1 to 3.6 (p<0.001). Complication rate was 24% (PJK 12%, infection 5%, neurological deficit 3%). Conclusion: PSO effectively corrects severe sagittal imbalance with acceptable complication rates.

test_papers.title: Short Segment Fixation versus Long Segment Fixation for Thoracolumbar Burst Fracture: A Meta-analysis
test_papers.abstract: Background: Optimal fixation strategy for thoracolumbar burst fractures remains controversial. Methods: Meta-analysis of 12 RCTs (856 patients) comparing short segment (≤2 levels above and below) vs long segment fixation. Results: No significant difference in VAS (MD -0.3, 95% CI -0.8 to 0.2, p=0.24) or ODI (MD -2.1%, 95% CI -5.3 to 1.1, p=0.19). Short segment had lower complication rate (15% vs 24%, OR 0.58, 95% CI 0.39-0.87, p=0.007) and shorter operative time (p<0.001). Long segment had lower reoperation rate (8% vs 15%, OR 1.98, 95% CI 1.18-3.32, p=0.009). Conclusion: Short segment fixation offers comparable clinical outcomes with fewer complications but higher reoperation rate.

test_papers.title: Surgical Outcomes of Spinal Metastasis: Decompression with Stabilization versus Decompression Alone
test_papers.abstract: Background: Optimal surgical strategy for spinal metastasis balances neurological recovery and mechanical stability. Methods: Retrospective analysis of 142 patients: decompression with stabilization (n=88) vs decompression alone (n=54). Mean survival was 18 months. Results: Neurological improvement rate was 78% in stabilization group vs 65% in decompression alone (p=0.04). VAS improved from 8.1 to 3.2 in stabilization vs 7.9 to 4.5 in decompression alone (p=0.02). Ambulation recovery was 71% vs 52% (p=0.01). Complication rate was 22% vs 18% (p=0.53). Median survival was 21 months vs 15 months (p=0.08). Conclusion: Decompression with stabilization provides better neurological recovery and ambulation in spinal metastasis patients.

test_papers.title: Biomechanical Comparison of OLIF and LLIF: Finite Element Analysis Study
test_papers.abstract: Background: OLIF and LLIF are lateral lumbar fusion techniques with different approach corridors. Methods: Finite element models of L4-5 fusion were created for OLIF51 and LLIF. Cage position, segmental lordosis, and biomechanical stress were analyzed under physiological loading. Results: OLIF showed 15% higher cage-endplate contact area (p<0.001). Segmental lordosis was 8.2° in OLIF vs 6.5° in LLIF (p=0.003). Peak von Mises stress in cage was lower in OLIF (42.3 MPa vs 51.7 MPa, p=0.002). Psoas muscle injury risk was lower in OLIF (anterior to psoas). Range of motion was similar (p=0.45). Conclusion: OLIF provides biomechanical advantages with better cage positioning and lower stress compared to LLIF.
